title: Distinct modes of non-canonical initiation regulate selective host versus viral mRNA translation during poxvirus-induced shutoff
practical_files: Per-sample gene count files in GEO supplementary RAW tar and sample FTP
role_in_expansion: Primary anchor dataset for host DHX/eIF/RACK1 secondary analysis
notes: Already analyzed by Park et al.; do not claim primary discovery

title: Vaccinia virus D10 has broad decapping activity that is regulated by mRNA splicing
role_in_expansion: Independent human Vaccinia host-shutoff dataset for DHX/translation-factor validation
notes: Good orthogonal host-shutoff comparison but not raw count-simple

title: Poxvirus attack of anti-viral defense pathways unleashes an effector-triggered NF-kB response [RNAseq_2]
role_in_expansion: Myxoma/poxvirus host RNA-seq matrix for cross-virus validation
notes: Direct count matrix; strong candidate for next pipeline module

title: Poxvirus attack of anti-viral defense pathways unleashes an effector-triggered NF-kB response
role_in_expansion: Compact Myxoma/poxvirus host RNA-seq validation set
notes: Direct count matrix; useful fast pilot

title: Poxvirus attack of anti-viral defense pathways unleashes an effector-triggered NF-kB response [RNAseq_MYXV_screen]
role_in_expansion: Screen-style MYXV dataset for perturbation-linked context
notes: Requires careful decoding of screen design before DGE

title: MeRIP-seq study of Vaccinia virus-infected Vero cell
practical_files: featurecounts plus DESeq2 and exomePeak files
role_in_expansion: Vaccinia time-course RNA-seq and m6A context for host translation-factor persistence
notes: Non-human primate ortholog mapping needed for direct DHX symbol comparison

title: Induction of an alternative 5' leader enhances translation of Inpp5e and resistance to oncolytic virus infection
role_in_expansion: Translation-efficiency comparison point for oncolytic virus resistance
notes: Mouse ortholog mapping and normalized-only caveats

title: Evaluating levels of Vaccinia viral mRNA translation
role_in_expansion: Viral-side translation context for leader/viral mRNA analysis
notes: Not host expression; useful for viral transcript side only

title: MURV-seq reveals genome-wide diversity of 5-prime termini and leader sequences across poxvirus transcripts
practical_files: Small RAW tar with wide text files
role_in_expansion: Very recent poxvirus 5-prime leader dataset for structured-leader/DHX29 hypothesis generation
notes: Potentially highest novelty but requires sequence/leader-specific methods

title: Transcriptome dynamics of mutualistic poxvirus infection of tropical fruit fly hosts
notes: Less directly relevant to human DHX29 and translation initiation

title: Dynamic transcriptome profiling dataset of vaccinia virus obtained from long-read sequencing techniques (GigaScience 2018)
notes: Viral-side and isoform-focused long-read; not a host count matrix; use for temporal viral-program framing

title: Time-Course Transcriptome Profiling of a Poxvirus Using Long-Read Full-Length Assay (Pathogens 2021)
notes: Characterizes viral kinetic gene classes/TSS-TES; viral-side not host-side; raw long-read not DGE-comparable

title: Deciphering Poxvirus Gene Expression by RNA Sequencing and Ribosome Profiling (Yang & Moss J Virol 2015)
public_evidence: PMID 25903347; PMC4468498; DOI 10.1128/JVI.00528-15; BioProject PRJNA280609 / SRA SRP056975
practical_files: 3 RNA-seq + 12 ribosome-profiling raw FASTQ (no GEO processed matrix); pipeline vendored at data/external/yang_moss_2015/
role_in_expansion: Host translation-over-time EXPANSION TARGET (mentor-flagged); pipeline READY pending network
notes: Run quantify_SRP056975.sh on a network machine; verify mock-vs-infected from ENA sample titles before adding the contrast to the meta-analysis

title: Spatio-temporal analysis of Vaccinia virus infection and host response dynamics using single-cell transcriptomics and proteomics (bioRxiv 2024)
public_evidence: bioRxiv 2024.01.13.575413; scRNA-seq GEO accession to confirm
practical_files: Single-cell metadata + HeLa proteomics vendored in data/external/matia2024/
role_in_expansion: INTEGRATED as external validation (cross-cell-type host shutoff + cross-modality protein concordance) via scripts/24
notes: Supplementary tables used directly; raw scRNA-seq count matrices (GEO) would enable full pseudobulk meta-analysis join
